Title: Arabidopsis DREB26/ERF12 and its close relatives regulate cuticular wax biosynthesis under drought stress condition

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transiently induced at 24 h under drought/dehydration stress; promoter activity in cotyledons, true leaves, primary roots, ovules within carpels, early embryo and suspensor; promoter activity increased in whole leaves under dehydration; protein mainly functions in epidermal/outer root and hypocotyl cells
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently induced at 24 h under drought/dehydration stress; promoter activity in cotyledons, true leaves, primary roots, ovules within carpels, early embryo and suspensor; promoter activity increased in whole leaves under dehydration; protein mainly functions in epidermal/outer root and hypocotyl cells
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and supports drought tolerance by strengthening cuticle barrier
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf surface cuticle/epicuticular wax of aerial organs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax biosynthesis, especially VLC-alkane branch
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CER1 and SHN3 promoters; positively regulates KCS2, CER26L, CER1, MAH, and SHN3 expression; negatively affects KCS16; little clear effect on cutin biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Drought stress; dehydration; likely early ABA-associated drought response
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Overexpression (35Spro::DREB26OX); dominant repression (35Spro::DREB26SRDX); qRT-PCR; GC-MS wax profiling; toluidine blue permeability assay; transmission electron microscopy; water loss assay; protoplast transient transactivation assay
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes cuticular wax accumulation, especially VLC-alkanes, thickens cuticle, reduces leaf permeability and water loss under dehydration stress
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DREB26/ERF12 is an early drought-inducible nuclear AP2/ERF regulator expressed mainly in epidermis-related tissues. 2. DREB26 overexpression increased total wax and VLC-alkanes, thickened cuticle, and improved water retention, whereas dominant repression caused thinner/disrupted cuticle and higher permeability. 3. DREB26 activated CER1 and SHN3 promoters and positively regulated wax biosynthetic gene expression, indicating a direct role in drought-responsive wax biosynthesis.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Early drought-induced positive regulator that helps initiate transcriptional cascade for VLC-alkane-rich cuticular wax accumulation
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drought/dehydration stress by withholding watering; long-day growth conditions for many assays
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S constitutive promoter for overexpression/repression and GFP fusion; native DREB26 promoter for GUS/GFP reporter assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Overexpression caused fewer rosette leaves and earlier flowering; repression caused growth retardation, chlorosis, anthocyanin accumulation, leaf adhesion, and late flowering
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for engineering drought tolerance through enhanced cuticular wax accumulation, but constitutive misexpression causes developmental penalties, so stress-inducible or epidermis-specific deployment would be preferable
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray analysis mentioned in supporting information; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DREB26 appears more specific to VLC-alkane biosynthesis than broad cutin regulation; stronger phenotype of SRDX lines than triple mutant suggests suppression of additional related AP2/ERFs

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transiently induced at 24 h under drought/dehydration stress; promoter activity in cotyledons, true leaves, primary roots and petals; leaf promoter activity increased under dehydration
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently induced at 24 h under drought/dehydration stress; promoter activity in cotyledons, true leaves, primary roots and petals; leaf promoter activity increased under dehydration
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and supports drought tolerance by strengthening cuticle barrier
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf surface cuticle/epicuticular wax of aerial organs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax biosynthesis, especially VLC-alkane branch
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CER1 and SHN3 promoters; contributes with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF14 to induction of wax biosynthetic genes under drought
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter::GUS expression analysis; GFP localization; protoplast transient transactivation assay; CRISPR/Cas9 single mutant generation and inclusion in erf12/13/14 triple mutant; qRT-PCR; GC-MS wax profiling of triple mutant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF14 to promote drought-inducible VLC-alkane accumulation and leaf water retention
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF13 is an early drought-inducible nuclear AP2/ERF closely related to DREB26/ERF12. 2. ERF13 activates CER1 and SHN3 promoters in protoplast assays. 3. Together with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF14, ERF13 is required for normal drought-inducible wax gene expression and VLC-alkane accumulation in the triple mutant.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant co-activator within the A5 AP2/ERF module controlling drought-responsive cuticular wax production
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drought/dehydration stress by withholding watering
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ERF13 promoter for GUS reporter; 35S constitutive promoter for GFP fusion and protoplast effector assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contribution to triple mutant growth reduction under drought; individual tradeoffs not resolved in detail
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential stacking target with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF14 for improving drought-protective wax formation, though redundancy suggests multigene engineering may be necessary
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray analysis mentioned in supporting information; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously reported to function in VLC-FA biosynthesis during lateral root development, indicating pleiotropy beyond drought wax regulation

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transiently induced at 24 h under drought/dehydration stress; promoter activity mainly in roots and immature stamen in buds; little detectable leaf promoter activity and little change under dehydration
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Transiently induced at 24 h under drought/dehydration stress; promoter activity mainly in roots and immature stamen in buds; little detectable leaf promoter activity and little change under dehydration
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduces non-stomatal water loss and supports drought tolerance by strengthening cuticle barrier
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf surface cuticle/epicuticular wax of aerial organs
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Cuticular wax biosynthesis, especially VLC-alkane branch
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates CER1 and SHN3 promoters; contributes with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF13 to induction of wax biosynthetic genes under drought
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Promoter::GUS expression analysis; GFP localization; protoplast transient transactivation assay; CRISPR/Cas9 single mutant generation and inclusion in erf12/13/14 triple mutant; qRT-PCR; GC-MS wax profiling of triple mutant
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts redundantly with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF13 to promote drought-inducible VLC-alkane accumulation and maintain leaf water content
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERF14 is a drought-responsive nuclear AP2/ERF family member with expression mainly in roots and reproductive tissues. 2. ERF14 activates CER1 and SHN3 promoters in protoplast assays. 3. Together with DREB26/ERF12 and ERF13, ERF14 contributes to drought-responsive wax gene expression and VLC-alkane accumulation, as shown by the triple mutant phenotype.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Redundant co-regulator in the A5 AP2/ERF module supporting drought-induced cuticular wax synthesis
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Soil drought/dehydration stress by withholding watering
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native ERF14 promoter for GUS reporter; 35S constitutive promoter for GFP fusion and protoplast effector assays
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Contribution to triple mutant drought-sensitive growth phenotype; individual tradeoffs not resolved in detail
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential component of multigene strategies to enhance drought-induced wax deposition, but redundancy and pleiotropic roles should be considered
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: Microarray analysis mentioned in supporting information; accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: ERF14 has reported roles in fungal defense gene regulation, indicating broader stress-function pleiotropy
